LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS LAURA SOLORANZO, JAIME RODRIGUEZ'S MOTION TO STAY. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STAY THE JULY 15 TRIAL IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BRINGING A MOTION IN DEPARTMENT 206, WHERE THE PRESIDING JUDGE DECIDES ALL MOTIONS TO CONTINUE TRIAL OR HAVING THAT EFFECT. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. NOT REPORTED. DEPUTY: NOT PRESENT. (302/RBU)

Defendants Laura Solorzano & Jaime Rodriguez'S Notice Of Motion To Stay
Off Calendar May-29-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Hearing re: DEFENDANTS LAURA SOLORANZO, JAIME RODRIGUEZ'S Motion To Stay.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants' motion to stay the July 15 trial is denied without prejudice to bringing a motion in Department 206, where the presiding judge decides all motions to continue trial or having that effect.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Deputy: Not Present. (302/RBU)
